figures.caption: Fig. 11. A comparison of the measured and estimated skew of bulk cable.
Fig. 10. a) The skew measurements setup, b) The propagation time delay difference ∆τ between the even and odd modes as function of frequency, c) Single-ended through S-parameters..

Fig. 2. (a) Schematic of the cross section of dual-extruded twinax cable with 26 AWG conductors and differing dielectric regions used to model asymmetry. (b) Simulated single-ended through and forward-coupling S-parameters for 0.5m and 1m dual-extruded twinax cables, showing the periodic resonances caused by even and odd mode propagation delays differences. (c) Unwrapped phase plots for common and differential modes, used to extract ∆τ for resonance frequency calculations.
Fig. 4. Intra-pair skew versus frequency for twinax cables of 0.5m and 1m length, showing the damped oscillatory behavior and zero-skew points predicted by (21) and (22).
